role: source-home preflight for KAG-owned local-subtree protocol law, substrate posture, owner-return routes, and future portable record contracts
home_rules: kag/ owns KAG source-home law and local-subtree protocol posture; it is not a payload warehouse.
Current manifests, generated read models, schemas, examples, scripts, tests, and mechanics remain in their existing owner homes until a reviewed KAG source family moves.
The only active source families in this preflight are the home route cards, this source-home manifest, and the local-subtree protocol.
Future local record classes are reserved vocabulary, not active directories, until schema, example, validator, pilot owner, and decision record exist.
Generated readers summarize KAG records; live graph, vector, embedding, runtime index, and cache state stay outside Git-tracked source-home truth.

branches.role: Root preflight branch for KAG source-home route law before portable record classes are active.
stop_lines: Do not split this branch into empty future-surface directories.
Do not treat source-home preflight as sibling /kag rollout readiness.
Do not move generated payloads, root manifests, or mechanic payloads here by symmetry.

families.meaning: Human and agent-facing entry law for the KAG source-home preflight.
stop_lines: Do not make route cards stronger than source refs, schemas, validators, or owner decisions.
Do not bury future rollout intent in prose without a decision route.

families.meaning: Machine-readable atlas for what kag/ currently owns, what it reserves, and which stronger owners must accept adjacent pressure.
stop_lines: Do not use the manifest as a generated reader.
Do not add active record families without real files, schemas, examples, and validators.

families.meaning: Preflight protocol for future repo-local KAG organs: source refs, portable record classes, storage posture, rollout order, and runtime-state exclusions.
stop_lines: Do not authorize sibling /kag directories from this protocol alone.
Do not infer graph edges without source refs or a declared derived method.
Do not treat local indexes as ranking, proof, memory acceptance, or routing authority.

reserved_future_surfaces.activation_condition: schema, example, validator, pilot owner, and decision record exist
reserved_future_surfaces.role: repo-local KAG organ manifest for source refs, record classes, generation routes, validators, storage posture, and freshness mode

reserved_future_surfaces.activation_condition: node schema, source-ref validator, and pilot owner exist
edge schema, relation policy, no-inference guard, and pilot owner exist
reserved_future_surfaces.role: bounded direct or explicitly derived edge records

reserved_future_surfaces.activation_condition: index contract forbids embedding, vector, runtime-search, and cache state
reserved_future_surfaces.role: lightweight lookup indexes over portable local KAG records
compact consumer read models derived from manifest-backed records

reserved_future_surfaces.activation_condition: receipt schema and closeout route exist
reserved_future_surfaces.role: generation, validation, freshness, and regrounding receipts

stronger_owner_routes.pressure: AoA organ constitution, center doctrine, or cross-repo owner law
ToS-authored source meaning, canon, or philosophy graph workbench
agent role meaning or runtime-seam role posture
scenario choreography or playbook bundle meaning
dispatch, route selection, or navigation authority
typed helper, SDK facade, or public package behavior
technique canon or source-lift method meaning
durable memory object, recall truth, or reviewed memory corpus
proof verdict, eval claim, or scoring authority
runtime service, graph database, vector index, cache, worker, or deployment state

stop_lines: No sibling /kag rollout from this preflight alone.
No live graph, vector, embedding, runtime search, or cache state in this source home.
No source-authored meaning transfer into KAG records.
No proof verdict, memory acceptance, ranking truth, or routing authority.
No empty future-surface directories without schema and validation.
